_comment_goos: Go's stdlib has no windows/loong64 combination, so we use goos=linux to keep TinyGo's runtime happy and rely on the llvm-target / linker steps to produce PE32+ output. Functionally this is fine: TinyGo with gc=leaking + scheduler=none + libc="" never touches libc or syscalls.
_comment_llvm_triple: loongarch64-pc-windows-gnu makes LLVM emit COFF/PE relocations rather than ELF. lld-link cannot then stitch the final BOOTLOONGARCH64.EFI (no /machine:loongarch64 in the COFF driver) — but go-coff/peln (`pectl link --machine loongarch64 …`) can.
_comment_features: +f,+d enables single/double-precision FP (mandatory for LP64D). +lsx enables 128-bit SIMD (Loongson SIMD eXtension) — present on every la464+ CPU since the architecture's launch.
